title: IBM Research shows two dozen CUGA agent apps on Hugging Face
friendlyTitle: IBM shared example apps for building AI agents
summary: IBM Research published a set of working CUGA examples showing how to build agentic apps with a lightweight harness that handles planning, tool execution, state, guardrails, provider switching, and multi-agent delegation. The examples are designed as small, readable FastAPI apps that developers can copy and adapt.
friendlySummary: IBM Research released many small example apps that show how its CUGA agent system works. The examples handle common agent needs like tools, memory, safety rules, and switching models.
whyItMatters: Agent quality increasingly depends on the harness around the model. Reusable examples lower the cost of moving from demos to governed, repeatable agent workflows that can run with different models and deployment choices.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Developers can copy these examples instead of building every part of an AI agent from scratch.
